D9 BiologyL5 EvaluateReference

Chameleon — Guanine Crystal Lattice Tuning

panther chameleons change colour by adjusting the spacing of guanine nanocrystals in their iridophores — Teyssier 2015 showed the lattice constant tunes from blue (relaxed) to red (excited)

Teyssier et al. 2015 used cryo-EM to show panther chameleon skin has two layers of iridophores with guanine crystals. The upper layer's lattice constant is tunable: relaxed skin → small spacing → blue; excited skin → larger spacing → green/yellow/red. Combined with pigment in chromatophores, the full visible range is accessible.

Drag iridophore lattice expansion; colour shifts blue → red.
